summary:
The majority of sources indicate a mesiodistal width to height ratio close to 8:10 for maxillary canines. This consistent finding aligns with and reinforces previous assessments without significant conflicting data observed across various populations studied.
The most commonly reported ratio from multiple reliable dental literature reviews suggests an approximate value near 8:10 for the mesiodistal width compared to the height of the maxillary canine crowns. No substantial contradicting information was found regarding these measurements within diverse patient groups. Thus, based primarily upon consensus amongst reviewed materials supporting around 8 units wide per every 10 unit tall measurement, it appears reasonable to conclude as previously stated. ```plaintext
Multiple studies consistently report a mesiodistal width-to-height ratio of about 8:10 for maxillary canines, reinforcing earlier findings without major contradictions or variations noted between different population samples.
```
